entry.title: Re: SAP MDK – Responsive layout support
entry.content:
Yes, the MDK pages use responsive controls that will layout based on the size of the device.   For example on a phone a control may render the caption on one line and the value on the second line in portrait but if using landscape render caption and value on the same line.  Another example is some sections support multiple columns when running on a tablet but not on a phone.

For the Section Button Table there is a Layout Type option that can be set to horizontal to get side by side buttons depending on the length of the button Title.  So depending on the number of buttons and the length of the Titles it will render side by side based on the space available before wrapping beneath the first buttons.

entry.title: Re: Launch Excel SAC Add-In file from SAC folder
entry.content: I have a link in my SAC story that triggers an Excel download. However, I'm experiencing unwanted navigation behavior: If "Open in new tab" is enabled: The link opens the Files folder in a new tab AND downloads the Excel file If "Open in new tab" is disabled: I'm redirected away from the story to the Files folder screen AND the Excel downloads Expected behavior: I want to remain on the current story page while the Excel file downloads in the background. Is there a configuration or workaround that allows the Excel to download without navigating away from or opening additional tabs/folders? I simply want users to stay on the story screen while the file downloads. Any guidance would be appreciated!

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK – Responsive layout support
entry.content: Thanks for the explanation, that makes sense. I have a couple of follow-up questions regarding this behavior. At the moment, the same screen looks fine on an iPhone Plus/Pro, but on smaller devices (e.g. iPhone 14 / standard), the content becomes longer vertically and the user needs to scroll to see all the information. Is there a recommended way in MDK to dynamically adjust the layout so that the same screen fits better across different phone sizes, or is this expected behavior and scrolling is unavoidable on smaller devices? Regarding the Section Button Table: we tried setting the Layout Type to Horizontal, and the buttons do appear side by side as expected. However, we are unable to make the buttons larger, it seems like their width is fixed. Is there a way to control or influence the button width, or is this also handled automatically by MDK based on the available space? If possible, could you also share a concrete example or sample app that demonstrates best practices for: handling layout differences across phone sizes, and configuring horizontal buttons effectively? Thanks in advance for the guidance!

entry.title: Re: Latest People Profile - Calibration History & Trend D...
entry.content:
Hi Jennifer @jgrossnicklaus43 

1 - This specific card cannot be renamed. https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/configuring-people-profile/configuring-predefined-cards

For the Calibration History card, you can only add/remove Facilitators and Discussed? fields, but anything else shows by default. Even if we disable the matrix views to display in the calibration history from the Calibration Template, that does not seem to hide the Matrix Rating column even if empty. 

2 - Trend Data Card

To rename Custom Rating 1, you can navigate to Manage Business Configuration and change the label for the sysOverall block (if this is the custom1 then look for sysOverallCustom1 under Background Elements.

The order sequence of the fields within the Trend Information cards cannot be changed. You can confirm on the link that I shared before, where all the specifications for each predefined card are documented. 

Best regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Event Enabling Idocs rapidly and almost effortlessly via BTP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration
entry.content:
1) Introduction

In the past we have published a couple of Blogs where we describe how to event enable Idocs via Event Enablement AddOn or Application Interface Framework. Another possibility with the least configuration on the ERP (ECC or S/4) System is the usage of the HTTP Port and SAP Cloud Integration. This Blog will demonstrate how to easily accomplish this + how to dynamically add Topic Values with the Help of the Partner Directory in SAP Cloud Integration.  

 

2) ERP Customizing

Let´s start with the Definition of the Logical System in Transaction SALE

I have defined this Logical System for the Connection to AEM via Cloud Integration

In SM59 I have already prepared a Destination to SAP Cloud Integration Iflow receiving the Idocs:

Of course later we´ll have to make sure that the Iflow Address and the endpoint in this Destination are the same. Based on this Destination we create a Port in WE21:

 

Then we´ll need a Partner Profile in WE20 with the Receiver Port we just created. Let´s say we want to distribute Vendor Master Data with CREMAS:

Also we´ll need a Distribution Model in BD64:

(Only the Receiver CPI_AEM relevant here)

That is all we have to do on the ERP! Let´s switch to the Cloud Integration.

 

2) SAP Cloud Integration

Static Topic

In my Github Repo I have provided a Template Iflow you can upload into your Cloud Integration Package 

First thing is to configure the Sender Address and Role:

 

In the Content Modifier Step we retrieve the Idoc Headers from the Payload:

Then, depending if the Idoc has a CIMTYP or not, we put together the Topic. 

With CIMTYP:

Header "Topic" = "${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_SNDPRN}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_MESTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_IDOCTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_CIMTYP}"

Without CIMTYP the Topic is filled like this: "${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_SNDPRN}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_MESTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_IDOCTYP}"

So if I have an CREMAS07 Idoc from my System T33, the Topic would be 

T33CLNT800/CREMAS/CREMAS07

If this is already enough for you, you can ignore or even remove the 2 Groovy Scripts after the Router in the Iflow.

The only Thing left of course is the Receiver Channel. Here we have to maintain the Connection Parameters

That´s it. You can deploy the Iflow!

Dynamic Topic with PartnerDirectory

If we want to include Parts of the Payload into the Topic, we´ll have to get it via Xpath. This could be easily done via Content Modifier, but to keep everything dynamic in one Iflow, I decided to implement 2 Groovy Scripts. 1st Script is retrieving the XPaths I set up for my Idoc Type in the Partner Directory. 2nd Script performs the XPath on the Body and puts together the Topic.

In order to enable this, i added the switch "GetDynamicTopicFromPartnerDirectoy" 

The Maintainence of the Idoc-specific XPaths happens in the Partner Directory. So in Monitor -> Integration and APIs -> Partner Directory

The PartnerID is always <MESTYP>.<IDOCTYP>.<CIMTYP> (if CIMTYP exists) or <MESTYP>.<IDOCTYP> (no CIMTYP). In my Case it is CREMAS.CREMAS07. I have defined these 3 XPaths

Test

Now let´s test the Scenario. I set the Log Level on the Iflow to "Trace" and set up a Consumer on Advanced Event Mesh TryMe Feature on Topic "T33CLNT800/>" 

 

Now let´s send a Vendor from the ERP System Transaction BD14:

Result:

Let´s check in WE05:

Now let´s check the Trace in SAP Cloud Integration Message Monitoring:

Here we can see at the Receiver Channel Step the dynamic Topic we produced:

Also on Advanced Event Mesh we can see the result in TryMe:

Dynamic Topic and Conversion with Custom Exit Iflow

Let´s say I want to convert the XML into a JSON and Filter out some Fields. For that I would use a custom exit, because that would be Idoc specific.

For this I added the Switch "CallCustomExit"

 

I configured the address to dynamically set the Address based on the Message- and Idoc Type, so here the complete Address would be "/IdocEventing/CREMAS/CREMAS07"

For the Custom Exit i added another Iflow into the GIT Repo:

 

For a Filter i choose to define a Message Mapping. You can use a different Step if you prefer. For a Mapping I need the XSD for this Idoc. This can be exported in the ERP System, Transaction WE61:

Now just import it into Cloud Integration

Create a Message Mapping:

and add it into your Mapping (Source and Target)

 

First I map all Fields with this Button:

Then I disable the Structures I don´t want to map

I disabled everything except EDI_DC40 and E1LFA1M. Then I saved and added the json Conversion to my Iflow:

Test

Let´s test again:

(Same Procedure as above, Send Vendor with BD14)

Result: Dynamic Topic is the same, Payload has been mapped and converted:

Pretty Printed in Notepad++:

That´s it! Happy Eventing!

entry.title: Re: Move to SAP S/4HANA with Selective Data Transition
entry.content:
This is an excellent and very timely breakdown of Selective Data Transition. The clarification between Shell Conversion and Mix & Match is especially helpful — many customers still treat SDT as a single method rather than a spectrum of options.

In the utility programs I’ve been part of, SDT has been most valuable when organizations need phased go‑lives or want to preserve specific process areas while modernizing others. Your point about balancing the reduced cutover risk against the operational complexity of running two live ERP systems is spot‑on. That trade‑off is often underestimated.

The reference to the updated Activate roadmap and the Business Transformation Center is also appreciated. These newer tools are becoming increasingly relevant for customers who want a more structured, lower‑risk SDT path.

Thanks for keeping this topic current — especially with the 2025 updates. It’s a great resource for teams evaluating their path to S/4HANA.

entry.title: How to configure eSignature with DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
SAP Fieldglass supports the needs of Buyers to have documents electronically signed with DocuSign as the eSignature provider.

In this blog, we’ll cover everything you need to know to configure an integration between DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass to support your eSignature workflow.

You can also download this information as a one-pager here.

Prerequisites & Required Resources

Before you get started, you must ensure that your company has at least one DocuSign account established:

  • Details from your DocuSign account are required to set up the integration between SAP Fieldglass and DocuSign
  • Contact your SAP representative if you have any questions

If your company has a DocuSign account, ensure the correct resources are engaged to support the setup of this integration:

  • A user with SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager access is required to create the DocuSign End Point
  • A user with SAP Fieldglass Administrator access is required to create a DocuSign eSignature Template, Activity Items and Checklists, and update User permissions 
  • A DocuSign Administrator is required to provide the relevant details from your account to create a DocuSign Template


Configuration Steps

Once the prerequisites and resources are in place, follow these steps to set up the integration between DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass:

  1. The first step is to raise a case with SAP Fieldglass to enable the company configuration: eSignature Provider – DocuSign
  2. Once the company configuration has been enabled, the SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager can create the DocuSign End Point
    • A DocuSign Administrator will need to provide the following values from your DocuSign account to configure the End Point:

      SAP Fieldglass End Point (mandatory fields)
      Required values from your DocuSign account

      End Point

      Account Base URI located under My Account Information

      Integrator Key

      Integrator Key for the SAP Fieldglass application is located under Apps and Integrator Keys

      •        Note: Before completing this step, SAP Fieldglass may need to be added by selecting Add App and Integration Key

      •        The DocuSign Administrator must add the value http://localhost to the Redirect URI field by selecting Edit > Additional Settings > Add URI

      User ID

      User ID located under My Account Information

      Private Key

      The DocuSign Administrator will need to generate the RSA key pair located under Service Integration > Generate RSA



  3. The next step is to request application consent for SAP Fieldglass to support eSignature workflows
    • Details on how to request application consent can be found on the DocuSign Developers portal > Using JSON Web Token (JWT) Grant page
    • The process involves constructing a URI with a base path for the application service (test or production), your requested scope, integration key, and redirect URI. It will look like the URI shown here:
    • https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth?response_type=code&scope=signature%20impersonation&client_id=YOUR_INTEGRATION_KEY&redirect_uri=http://localhost
    • The URI needs to be copied and paste into a browser to accept and allow access

  4. Next, the DocuSign Administrator creates a DocuSign Template in DocuSign
    • DocuSign Templates allow you to standardize the signing process by predefining certain information like recipient roles, recipient emails, signature types, and signing fields

  5. The SAP Fieldglass Administrator then creates a DocuSign eSignature Provider Template in SAP Fieldglass
    • This step essentially provides the link to your company's templates in DocuSign
  6. Any users who will be allowed to electronically sign documents will need to be identified and have the User can be Selected to Electronically Sign a Document permission enabled
    • This permission can be enabled directly on the user profile page or updates can be completed in bulk using the User Upload connector

  7. Configure your company's eSignature workflow with Activity Items and (optional) Activity Checklist
    • Your desired contract workflow will determine which Activity Items need to be setup
      • For example, a Buyer may require an initial step, such as document validation, prior to triggering the DocuSign integration
    • At a minimum, an Activity Item with the Completion Type: Initiate eSignature Process must be configured to kick-off the eSignature workflow
    • Multiple workflows can be setup and used for different types of documents that require electronic signature


Helpful Resources

  • Watch the Configuration Manager: How to configure eSignature with DocuSign tutorial video for detailed, step-by-step instructions on how to set up the integration 
  • Download this one-pager for a quick overview of the prerequisites, required resources, and steps to configure eSignature with DocuSign

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q1 2026
entry.content:
As we move toward the end of January we take a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in SAP Analytics Cloud for QRC1 2026. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.  Let's dive in!

New Chart Types

To kick off the year we are expanding the native visualization capabilities available with the introduction of new Pareto and area chart types designed to simplify advanced visualization and analysis. The Pareto chart automatically performs 80/20 analysis using built‑in running‑total logic and allows the addition of comparison measures without affecting the core calculation. The new area chart enhances trend exploration through more intuitive visual breakdowns.

Together, these new chart types reduce reliance on custom widgets, streamline dashboard creation, and mark the beginning of a broader roadmap to deliver more out‑of‑the‑box visualizations that elevate analytical flexibility and user experience.

 

Story Versioning

Staying with Story enhancements this quarter we introduce a new story versioning framework enables designers to create, manage, and restore up to ten major versions of a story, providing greater control over iterative development. Story Designers can access version history, open previous versions in a read-only format, restore them, or save them as new artifacts, supporting safe experimentation without risking published content.

This capability ensures transparency in change tracking and simplifies collaboration across teams.

As the foundational phase of a larger multi artifact versioning roadmap, it establishes the technical basis for extending version control to additional SAP Analytics Cloud asset types in future releases.

 

 

Comment Management

We know how important collaboration is for our customers, so this quarter we have introduced a new comment management dashboard that enables a centralized and streamlined way to to manage comments across SAP Analytics Cloud models and versions. This gives Administrators visibility into comment statistics, including threads, data‑point comments, and dimension comment, enabling more effective governance and auditability.

Bulk actions, such as deleting comments at a version or model level and copying comments across versions within Model, simplify administrative workflows and help maintain clean, compliant environments.

 

 

My Metrics for KPIs monitoring: Alerting 

Continuing to enhance our "My Metrics" framework introduced introduced at the end of 2025 with lots of new features for an even more personalized experience. These include:

  • Subscribe or unsubscribe to metrics reports for continuous change tracking
  • Set report frequency by day, week, or month
  • Share reports with other users or teams 
  • Receive reports by e-mail or through the notification center 

 

 

Live data connectivity to Snowflake

In this release we are excited to announce that SAP Analytics Cloud has extended its external live data access for planning capable models to Snowflake.

This connection reduces data loading overhead, simplifies model configuration, and lowers total cost of ownership by allowing customers to work directly with Snowflake data as a live external version. Once the data access agent is enabled, users can create planning capable models based on Snowflake sources, benefiting from the same performance considerations and modeling flexibility available for existing live connections such as Google BigQuery.

 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC1 2026 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer  and the What's New Viewer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

entry.title: Error While Filtering Goods Receipts by "DocTime"
entry.content:
Hello,

   I have a problem and I was wondering if anyone may have a suggestion.

Problem: I am trying to filter goods receipt forms that were created after a particular date and time. I'm using SAP's Service Layer REST API.

 Here is my GET request:

GET InventoryGenEntries?$filter=DocDate ge '2025-12-12T14:00:00Z' and DocTime ge '2025-12-12T14:00:00Z'

The expected response should be a nice json stream with a collection of Goods Receipt forms. Instead, I get the following json:

{
    "error" : {
        "code" : 299,
        "message" : {
            "lang" : "en-us",
            "value" : "Internal server error."
        }
    }
}

I already tried all the possible date and time formats documented in the SAP official Service Layer guide. Best case scenario I get a "Query string error - the given value('90000') of property 'DocTime' is of invalid time format", worst case scenario I get an "Internal server error."

And then, I found this KB: 2596415 - Error While Filtering Marketing Documents by "DocTime"

https://me.sap.com/notes/2596415/E

That KB is from 8 years ago, and a previous version of SAP. Plenty of patches have been published ever since.

Here is my environment: SAP Business One 10 sp 2505, SQL 2016 - fresh from three months ago.

Any help is greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Train-the-Trainers: SAP S/4HANA Global Bike 4.3
entry.content:
Join our exclusive 4-day Train-the-Trainers workshop designed especially for SAP educators and professors who want to deepen their knowledge of SAP S/4HANA and enhance the way they teach enterprise process management.

This hands-on training will be led by Marcela Revilla, Senior SAP Consultant Instructor, a highly experienced professional with multiple SAP certifications and extensive expertise in SAP education and implementation projects.

Throughout the workshop, participants will explore how key SAP S/4HANA modules work together within the Global Bike 4.3 environment, focusing on real-world business processes and end-to-end integration.

Who is this event for?

This event is designed exclusively for professors and are interested in academic and professional development in SAP and enterprise systems.

What will you learn?

Participants will gain a comprehensive understanding of the following SAP S/4HANA modules:

SAP SD – Sales and Distribution

  • Master Data Management: Managing customers, materials, and pricing conditions
  • Pre-Sales and Sales Order Processing: Quotations, sales orders, and availability checks
  • Delivery and Billing Management: Best practices for shipping and invoicing
  • Credit Management and Customer Service: Credit limits and after-sales processes

SAP MM – Materials Management

  • Procurement and Inventory Management: Optimizing purchasing and stock control
  • Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Automating demand forecasting and procurement needs
  • Invoice Verification: Streamlining payment processes

SAP PP – Production Planning

  • Demand Planning and MRP: Forecasting demand and calculating material needs
  • Master Data and Production Orders: Configuring materials, routings, and production orders
  • Execution, Control, and Integration: Managing material flows and production costs

SAP WM – Warehouse Management

  • Inventory and Stock Movement Management: Real-time control of goods movements
  • Storage Location Strategies and Handling Units: Optimizing space and batch control
  • Integration and Cross-Docking: Connecting WM with other SAP modules

Event Details

  • Dates: April 13, 14, 15, and 16
  • Time: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM (GMT-5)
  • Format: Live online workshop

Each day will focus on one SAP module:

  • April 13 – SAP Sales and Distribution (SD)
  • April 14 – SAP Materials Management (MM)
  • April 15 – SAP Production Planning (PP)
  • April 16 – SAP Warehouse Management (WM)

Benefits of Participating

  • End-to-end understanding of SAP S/4HANA logistics and operational processes
  • Practical insights for teaching and classroom application
  • Live interaction with an experienced SAP instructor
  • Opportunity to ask questions and exchange experiences with other educators

How to Register

Registration is required to participate.
Sign up now to secure your spot and be part of this exclusive learning experience for SAP educators. 

Don’t miss this opportunity to elevate your SAP teaching methods and empower your students with real-world SAP S/4HANA knowledge. We look forward to having you with us!

entry.title: Security Domain is not defaulted as per Logged-in user’s SD during Item / Class creation in SF LMS
entry.content:
Product: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System (LMS)

  • Issue Description:
    During learning creation activities in SF LMS (e.g., Item creation or Class creation), the Security Domain is not automatically fetched from the system as per logged-in user’s assigned Security Domain. The user is required to manually select the Security Domain every time.

Current Behavior:

  • Security Domain must be manually selected during learning creation

Expected Behavior:

 Security Domain should be automatically fetched based on:

  • Logged-in user’s assigned Security Domain

Question / Request:

  • Is this a known limitation in SF LMS?
  • Is there any configuration or best practice to default the Security Domain?

entry.title: Instructor ID not auto-populated / no validation against related user in SF LMS
entry.content:
During Instructor assignment or creation in SF LMS, the Instructor ID is not automatically populated based on the related user. Additionally, the system allows selection of a mismatched Instructor ID without any error or warning.

Expected:

Is this a known limitation, configurable, or should it be raised as an enhancement request?

entry.title: ‌🇳🇿 ‌Webinar: KiwiSaver
entry.content:
For the first time in many years, KiwiSaver is experiencing legal changes that impact payroll. The most notable change is an increase in the default contribution rates to 3.5% and the introduction of a new Temporary Rate Reduction process. These changes will require updates to your payroll system, and certain customer actions will be necessary to adjust both employee and employer contribution rates.

During this session, we will detail the legal changes and explain the updates released by SAP to accommodate these new requirements. We will also outline the necessary steps to update contribution rates for April 1, 2026.

Key Takeaways:

  • Gain an understanding of the new KiwiSaver requirements.
  • Learn about the SAP updates supporting the new Temporary Rate Reduction and understand how the infotype records should be maintained. 
  • Learn how to utilize an existing SAP program to update KiwiSaver records to the new contribution rates. 
  • Have the opportunity to ask any questions about these changes.

Who Should Attend:
All SAP NZ Payroll customers and partners are welcome to join.  

The session will be recorded and stored in the New Zealand Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization WorkZone group afterwards.

Please register your attendance so that you can be advised if there is any last minute change to scheduling.

REGISTRATION LINK

entry.title: Re: Lumira in BI2025
entry.content: According to the SAP BusinessObjects/Lumira compatibility matrix (SAP KB 3017689) Lumira 2025 is expected in "Q1-2026" so customers potentially still have a few months to wait. The same KB states that BI 2025 SP0 patch 8, which was released last week, is the first version of BI 2025 that will be compatible with Lumira 2025. So customers should at least put BI 2025 in a sandbox and begin the rest of their upgrade planning and testing.

entry.title: ‌🇳🇿‌ Webinar: NZ Payroll Tax Year Changes for April 1, 2026 (Non-KiwiSaver)
entry.content:
SAP is introducing several updates for the new tax year, alongside the KiwiSaver changes. These updates include an increase in the ACC threshold and rate, and more notably, new functionality designed to identify and refund overcalculated child support deductions or donation tax credits due to overpayments. 

The primary focus of this session will be to thoroughly explain this new functionality, ensuring users understand how and when to utilize it. Additionally, we will discuss a pending technical change to pay day filing. 

Please note, this session will not address the KiwiSaver changes effective from April 1, 2026. These will be covered in a dedicated KiwiSaver webinar. 

Key Takeaways:

  • Understand the new tax year rate changes.
  • Learn how AI could potentially assist in tax testing.
  • Recognize when an override may be necessary for either a retrospective child support deduction or a donation tax credit. 
  • Learn how to override a retrospective child support deduction or extinguish a donation tax credit for a prior pay period.
  • Gain an overview of upcoming changes to certificate management for pay day filing.
  • Have the opportunity to ask any questions about these changes.

Who Should Attend:
This session is open to all SAP NZ Payroll customers and partners.  

The session will be recorded and stored in the New Zealand Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization WorkZone group after the event.

Please register your attendance so that you can be advised if there is any last minute change to scheduling.

entry.title: Implement association to standard entity as Object Value Selector in custom service
entry.content:
Background

A Standard OVS allows users to select data from a popup dialog, typically linked (associated) to a standard object (e.g., Customer, Employee). For a Custom UI generated by Custom Services metadata must define the association and the service implementation must provide necessary logic for the OVS to work and show value on UI.

For example, let's say we have created an external service for "Project Order" using the SAP CAP framework and deployed it to SAP BTP. Within this service, we set up an association to the standard entity “Account”, allowing an Account to be linked to a single project order. We will walk through the necessary adjustments in metadata and the external service implementation to make this work.

Note: This blog focuses solely on the steps for adding standard value helps via OVS and does not cover the entire custom service creation process. Additionally, in this example, the external service is built using SAP CAP and deployed on SAP BTP.

Step: Create Destination in BTP.

This destination is used in the CAP service to query the standard service (in our example – Account) from an external one.
Provide all mandatory details along with authentication information in the destination settings. The URL should be the SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 system URL.

Please Note: Make sure the destination is configured correctly—this ensures secure and seamless connectivity between your CAP-based service and the standard entity exposed by your SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 system.

 

Step: Update Package.json

Create an object in the package.json file for Account service, as shown below. Please note that credentials are provided using the destination created in the previous step.

 

 

Note:

  • Replace " custom-service-association-blog" with the actual name of the destination you set up in BTP.
  • This configuration tells CAP to use the BTP destination (with all authentication/connection details) whenever your service refers to the Product Service.

 

Manually add dependencies if they are not included by default.

 

 

Dependencies such as "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client" and "@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience" are required when calling the SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 API. Ensure these packages are added to your project to enable seamless API integration.

 

Step: DB schema

Your database schema should include a UUID field to store the unique identifier (UUID) of the selected instance from the standard associated entity.
In this example, we use a accountUUID     field to hold the UUID of the chosen Account.

Add a composition field to your entity as shown in example below.

 

accountUUID      : UUID;

        account           : Composition of Account

                                on account.ID = accountUUID;

entity Account {

    key ID : UUID;

        accountID : UUID;

        displayId : String;

        @description name      : String(255);

}

Based on the above schema, the corresponding metadata JSON—which will be uploaded in V2—will be defined in the subsequent step.

 

Step: Add Logic in Service JS File

When a value is selected from the OVS (Object Value Selector) in the V2 UI, the UUID of the selected entity say ,product is passed to your external service.

Your service needs to:

  1. Store the received UUID in your custom entity.
  2. Return an object with values in the response structure.
    This object should contain:
    • The UUID of the selected product- (“accountID” - as defined in DB schema example)
    • The displayId (“displayId”- as defined in DB schema example)
    • The name (or a formatted name)(“name”- as defined in DB schema example)

Please note you can give any name for parameters here.

Example Response structure:

" Account ": {

        " accountID ": "1cf83580-e55d-11eb-bcf2-399a10fb1108",

        "displayId": "AHT_75",

        "name": " AHT COMBI"

    }

 

When a record is selected from an OVS (Object Value Selector) in the V2 application, the request typically contains only the Account’s UUID. However, the UI expects to display a user-friendly value like the formatted name or ID—not just the raw UUID. This response payload ensures the UI can display meaningful and easily understood information for the selected UUID.

 

After storing the selected UUID from the OVS, your service must fetch the account details (formatted name/displayID) from V2 API and enrich the response object accordingly. Depending on your scenario, you may call the V2 API individually for each UUID or use a batch call for efficiency.

Therefore, for POST and UPDATE operations, your service must enrich the response with these display fields.
If this logic is omitted, only the UUID will be shown on the UI, resulting in a poor user experience.

This logic needs to be added after DB “READ”.

Please find sample snippet below for logic to be added in JS file for DB operations:

 

this.after('READ', ‘ProjectOrders’, async (ProjectOrders, req) => {

       console.log("After.Read for ProjectOrders was started");

        // Skip if there are no ProjectOrders

        if (!ProjectOrders || ProjectOrders.length === 0) {

            return ProjectOrders;

        }

 

        // Enrich account details based on a V2 API call

        try {

 

              // Get Account details and add to response

            const accountApi = await cds.connect.to("Account.Service");

            const requestList2 = [];

 

            // forming batch call

            ProjectOrders?.forEach((po, index) => {

              if (!(po.account && po.account.accountID)) return;

                let accountV2EndPoint = `/sap/c4c/api/v1/account-service/accounts/${po.account.accountID}?$select=displayId,id,formattedName`;

                requestList2.push({

                    "id": 'accountV2_' + index++,

                    "url": accountV2EndPoint,

                    "method": "GET"

                })

            });

            const accountDataBatchResp = await accountApi.send({

                method: "POST",

                path: `$batch`,

                headers: {

                    "Content-Type": "application/json",

                },

                data: {

                    "requests": requestList2

                }

            });

            accountDataBatchResp.responses.forEach((eachAccDtl, index) => {

                if (eachAccDtl?.body?.value) {

                    ProjectOrders [index]['account'] = {

                        id: eachAccDtl.body.value.id,

                        name: eachAccDtl.body.value.formattedName,

                        displayId: eachAccDtl.body.value.displayId

                    };

                    console.log("Account response reached. Some values: "+eachAccDtl.body.value.displayId+" "+eachAccDtl.body.value.formattedName);

                }

            })

            return ProjectOrders;

        } catch (err) {

            return req.reject("Error Occured. "+err);

        }   

   })

 

 

For a particular entity, please check which attributes corresponds to description type field like formatted name/name.

Multiple ways to find attribute information – From openAPI or from Extensibility Administration setting (Hack!) from Sales are Service Cloud V2 application.

Like below:

 

Note:  In example external DB is storing only Account UUID, search or filter query on Account Name/Display ID will not work . Data replication for Display ID and Description fields is a prerequisite for sorting, filtering, and search to function. If data is not replicated, these operations will not work, and an appropriate error (HTTP 501) should be returned In case there is need to have filter/search on these fields, you need to include in external service DB and save the values.But, if there is change within V2 on these fields, say Account Name is changed, external DB will not know and continue having old value. This require additional implementation. Hence in example, only UUID is stored which is not updatable.

 

Step: Update Metadata Json

Metadata JSON should have corresponding entity field as defined in external service.

As per example entity field would look like:

{

                    "name": "account",

                    "dataType": "OBJECT",

                    "creatable": true,

                    "updatable": true,

                    "nullable": true,

                    "sortable": false,

                    "filterable": false,

                    "searchable": false,

                    "label": "Account",

                    "objectDefinition": [

                        {

                            "name": "accountID",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": true,

                            "updatable": true,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": false,

                            "filterable": false,

                            "searchable": false,

                            "label": "Account ID",

                            "dataFormat": "UUID",

                            "descriptionAttribute": "name",

                            "keyType": "FOREIGN",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "targetAttribute": "id",

                                "targetEntity": "sap.ssc.md.accountservice.entity.account",

                                "keyGroup": "account",

                                "targetService": "sap.ssc.md.service.accountService"

                            }

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "displayId",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": true,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "displayId": true,

                            "label": "Account Display ID",

                            "dataFormat": "ALPHANUMERIC",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "displayId",

                                "keyGroup": "account"

                            }

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "name",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": true,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "label": "Account Name",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "name",

                                "keyGroup": "account"

                            }

                        }

                    ]

                },

 

Lets break down this metadata.

  • Here the name of field is “account” same we had defined in external service DB field. DataType  should be “OBJECT”
  • To make field editable on UI and show value help, please make sure “createable” and “updatable” is set to true.
  • Object has 3 subfields – “accountID”,” displayId” and “name” respectively as per defined in external service.
  • Here, Account UUID (from V2) corresponds to “accountID” field. "keyType": "FOREIGN".

Please note the “dataformat” : UUID.

Add “descriptionAttribute: name”, (navigation happens based on UUID when OVS value link is clicked ,since we are showing UUID on UI, this attribute is set to indicate corresponding description field  )

  • “objectReference” parameter contains the associated entity information. In this case, its standard Accounts entity.

“keygroup” : “account” //Any name.

So, “targetAttribute” : “id” // UUID field from Account ID. As shown in Extensibility Admin screenshot above.

“targetEntity”  and “targetService” information can be taken from Extensibility Administration setting as shown below.

 

Similarly, for other fields, kindly note the fields attributes and have similar in metadata.

At runtime, OVS will display like :

 

Once value is selected, request payload would look like:

Response Payload:

UI:

Related Help Document:

Custom Services

entry.title: Re: Why two different buttons for our own account?

I am so sorry for the late reply now.

To start with the easy part, the comment section. We are aware of the "not great" user experience of adding comments. Changing it is unfortunately not straight forward, so you would have to publish a comment using the one-line UI, then go to edit your comment to get to the full editor. 

The Salesforce community also has a double log/two header concept, so we are not alone 🙂 
Not that it excuses the confusing navigation, which we are working on. 
You can find a little more information from the team behind the SAP Profile in this post: https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/new-in-sap-profile-your-recent-activity-feed/ba-p/14233366

entry.title: Seeking Guidance: UI State Persistence in RAP Unmanaged Query after Intent-Based Navigation
entry.content:
I’m currently working on a custom application using an Unmanaged RAP Query where we’ve implemented Intent-Based Navigation (IBN) to various standard SAP apps.

While the forward navigation works as expected, we are encountering an issue with the "Return" flow. When navigating back from the standard app to our custom RAP app, the UI state is lost—the filters are cleared, and the previously queried data is gone. It behaves as a fresh launch rather than a return to the previous state.

Standard SAP apps typically maintain this context (Pagination/Filter selections) via Inner App State or Selection Variant persistence.

Could anyone provide insight on how to properly implement state handling in an Unmanaged RAP context? Specifically:

  1. Are there specific annotations or manifest settings required to enable Component/UI State persistence?

  2. Does the Unmanaged Query require a specific implementation to "re-read" the navigation context upon return?

Looking forward to your expert advice!

Best regards,

Rowie

entry.title: Re: Payment Method isnot flowing from Vendor Master to FB60
entry.content: We experience the same issue.  However we run F110 separately and specifically for one payment method.  We do not mix different payment methods in a single F110 payment run.  Therefore the invoice document MUST have the payment method filled or else it will not get selected in the F110 proposal.  Payment runs (F110) are run separate per company code and per payment method. Reason:  payment method "E" outputs an "ACH" HTML File to go to Bank.  Payment method "C" creates spool output to go to printer (to print checks). Payment methods shouldn't be mixed in a single F110 proposal/payment run..

entry.title: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed & Recredited – Has Anyone Experienced This?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to check if anyone else has experienced a similar situation with the new system-based practical certification exams, particularly C_IEE2E – SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – End-to-End Business Processes for SAP Business Suite.

My situation:

  • I completed the C_IEE2E practical exam on 25 January 2026.

  • The exam status remained “Under Review” for several days (despite results usually being available shortly after completion).

  • I contacted SAP Certification Support.

  • I later received a response stating that:

    • They cannot provide detailed information on why the attempt was unsuccessful.

    • The certification is currently in a pilot phase, and detailed score reports are not yet available.

    • My attempt has been closed and recredited.

While I appreciate the recredit, I am now a bit unsure about the next best step.

My questions to the community and SAP team:

  1. Has anyone else faced a similar situation with C_IEE2E or other system-based practical exams?

  2. If your attempt was recredited, did you:

    • Retake the same exam immediately?

    • Wait for process improvements or clearer guidance?

  3. Are there known stability or evaluation issues with the pilot practical exams that candidates should be aware of?

  4. Would you recommend proceeding with a retake now, or waiting until the enhanced score reporting process is fully available?

Additionally, I am planning to take the following certifications soon:

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Management Accounting

Given this experience, I would really value advice on whether it’s better to:

  • Continue with these certifications now, or

  • Pause until the practical exam processes are more mature.

I’m sharing this to learn from others’ experiences and to get guidance from SAP experts or community members who may have gone through something similar.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: KW407: No prices could be determined for Activity Typ...
entry.content:
Good day @HuntonDavi11094 

Thank you for your question.

That error highlights that costing run did not find any valid activity price or cost rate for the specific combination of controlling area, your cost area, activity type and the planning context your valuation variant strategy is trying to use.

Note that uploading planned costs through import Financial Plan Data and planned quantities through Service Planning does not automatically guarantee that an activity price exists for costing. So, depending on your valuation strategy, the system might require:

  • Plan cost rate you manually maintained or
  • A cost rate that is calculated by the system. See Cost Center Activity Cost Rate Calculation for more insights.

If neither of the two is available in the ways the sequence expects it, the error you got reflects.

If neither is available in the way the strategy sequence expects, you get KW407.

Additionally, I suggest you:

  1. Validate your valuation variant strategy sequence used for activity prices. Make sure you are not using a plant specific variant that has higher priority than the global one. Your plan category must be PLN for the valuation variant. You mentioned PLN, still confirm it is set in the valuation variant used by your plant. See KBA 3318691 for more details.
  2. Your strategy might try to average the whole fiscal year, which requires the cost center to be valid on the fiscal year start date. If the cost center validity starts later in the year, the system can fail to fetch the rate and still display KW407. That is a known restriction in public cloud. In that case, i suggest you:
  • Change the strategy to plan price for the period, or
  • Extend the cost center validity so it is valid from the fiscal start year date. KBA 3589260 provides some insights.
  1. Ensure you have a cost rate in the correct place for your scenario. Because of your cost center category is C, also check whether you should maintain cost rates with the cost rates framework apps. See See Manage Cost Rates - Services and Manage Cost Rates - Plan for more details

To answer your questions:

Are there any additional prerequisites for activity price determination in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Yes, the key ones are:

  • The costing variant must point to a valuation variant whose activity strategy sequence can actually find a rate. This was elaborated above.
  • If the strategy averages the whole year, the cost center validity must cover the fiscal year start date. Also elaborated above.
  • A plan cost rate must exist either through manual maintenance or through system price calculation approach but depends on your strategy.

Are there known restrictions where planned activity prices are not read during costing?

Yes, the most common one in public cloud is the Average of all fiscal year periods strategy combined with cost center validity not starting at fiscal year start, which can then cause the error KW407 you experienced even when you think rates exist. Also, if you have maintained a plant specific valuation variant, it overrides the global valuation variant and can point to a different strategy than you expect.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Planned Independent Requirements for determining targ...
entry.content:
Hello @AngelT

Based on your screenshot, your PIR exist. So, the error you are seeing is not about the quantities you maintained. You seem to have a key mismatch. In your case, the most likely mismatch is MRP Area. PIRs are on MRP  Area 5540, while Rapid Replenishment is reading plant level. Add 5540 to MRP Area run and re test, then confirm it through message details.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Student Competition 2026
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the SAP Analytics Cloud Student Competition 2026, an analytics challenge open to universities across Latin America, Spain, and Portugal. Taking place in March 2026, this competition is designed to foster creativity, analytical thinking, and hands-on experience through the development of business dashboards using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

This initiative brings together students and professors to apply real-world analytics concepts, transform data into insights, and showcase innovative data storytelling skills.

 

Who Can Participate?

  • Teams of 4 to 5 undergraduate or graduate students

  • Each team must be led by one or two professors

  • Participation is open to universities from Latin America, Spain, and Portugal

  • Registrations must be completed individually

 

Challenge Focus

Participants will work with the Global Bike 4.3 dataset, analyzing key business areas such as sales, costs, and product performance.
The main objective is to design innovative and insightful business dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud, using comparative and trend-based visualizations and key metrics such as revenue, costs, and margins.

 

Registration Information

To register, teams must provide:

  • Team Name

  • University

  • Lead Professor (full name)

  • Lead Professor’s Email Address

  • Assistant/Co-Professor (optional)

Registration deadline:
Sunday, March 1st, 2026
(Registrations will not be accepted after this date.)

 

Event Structure & Key Dates

SAP Analytics Cloud Workshop

March 9, 10, and 11

Hands-on training sessions focused on:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud fundamentals

  • Data modeling

  • Dashboard design best practices

These sessions provide a solid technical foundation to prepare participants for the challenge.
Recommended level: intermediate knowledge of SAC.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Challenge – Opening Session

March 13

A session exclusively for professors, aimed at:

  • Presenting the competition objectives and rules

  • Explaining evaluation criteria

  • Introducing the Global Bike 4.3 dataset

  • Clarifying expectations and the professor’s role as facilitator

This session ensures professors are fully aligned and prepared to guide and support their student teams throughout the competition.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Competition Day

March 18

Official start of the competition.
Teams will:

  • Build and submit their dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud

  • Apply analytics, creativity, and storytelling to solve the challenge

Awards ceremony will take place on the same day.

 

Evaluation Criteria

Dashboards will be evaluated based on:

  • Analytical quality

  • Clarity of insights and communication

  • Creative and effective use of SAP Analytics Cloud tools

In addition, the SAP University Alliances community will participate by voting on dashboards, focusing on visual design and presentation.

 

Certificates & Recognition

  • All participants will receive a Certificate of Participation

  • Members of the top three winning teams will receive an official Digital Badge

 

Join Us!

This competition is a unique opportunity for students and professors to demonstrate analytical expertise, creativity, and innovation using SAP Analytics Cloud in an academic and collaborative environment.

We look forward to seeing your ideas come to life and wish all participants the best of luck!

Register here

Let’s shape the future of analytics together.

entry.title: Re: Where-used list of method with code line
entry.content: You may use the class CL_ABAP_COMPILER to know the exact line (and other CL_ABAP_COMP_* classes). It's parsing the source code. You may use for instance the method GET_SINGLE_REF to find out at which line(s) a desired method YOURCLASS=>YOURMETHOD is invoked, and the parameter P_FULL_NAME will be \TY:YOURCLASS\ME:YOURMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Need to know how can Return Revenue GL can be assigne...
entry.content:
Hello @abulhasnat

Regarding what “rule” you should use?

It is important to note that condition exclusion rules do not decide based on OR vs CBAR. They only decided after both conditions are found. So, you cannot solve “OR uses PPR0, CBAR uses ZINV” by an exclusion rule alone.

What you must do in standard pricing is to make sure only one of the two conditions is determined per scenario. Then the exclusion group becomes a safety net. The standard way is to differentiate by key fields in the condition record not BAdI.

I have some questions:

Are both of your condition types in the same pricing procedure?

Did you maintain condition records for PPR0 for standard categories?

Did you maintain condition records for ZINV only for the return item category that is used by CBAR?

 

Regarding LR2 --> G2, i suggest:

  • You stay with pricing based account key separation or you populate the custom field in billing by using Custom Logic.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Approval required not defaulted as yes along with App...
entry.content: @DINTPC, it can be set via System Administration > Configuration > Global Application Settings > "Global Application Settings" tab > "Approval Process Settings" area > "Default Internal Approval Process ID" field. It'll reflect on the "Approval Process" and "Withdraw Approval Process" fields (KBA 2280920).

entry.title: Re: Hi @Lakhsmipathi
Re: Role assignment of Admin not automated as per Locatio...
entry.content: @DINTPC, I may not have fully understood the situation, but here’s my comment. When a Learning User who has a related Learning Administrator account is inactivated, their admin account is automatically locked (KBA 2430232). In general, IDs cannot be changed, and entities cannot be deleted due to system integrity and restrictions (see KBA 2253851).

entry.title: Multiple Openings on Requisition and separate positions: How are others handling this?
entry.content:
We currently use position management, as well as the recruiting module.  We  have a 1:1 relationship between position and employee (there are no multi-incumbent positions).

We do however post requisitions with multiple openings. We then create additional position numbers to account for the additional openings.  Example: Position 1200 could have 3 openings, so we would create position 1201,  and 1203.

When someone has been hired into the requisition, they would automatically be placed into position 1201, and then additional hires would need the position to be manually overridden during the onboarding/new hire data entry step.

Our Recruiting team is currently putting notes in the requisition comments section, to notify our HR Operations team on which position to use for a new hire, but the step is frequently getting missed, and we our HR Ops team doesn't always know which position to use. 

Does anyone else use this same practice, or have advice on how best handle this type of process?

entry.title: Re: The consequences of implementing strictly Shift-left architectures
entry.content:
@appel_solar_dk  Thanks again for your comments. Maybe to add that the article/thought experiment is not only about SAP products, but my intention was to be more general, as I work not only with SAP anymore and I observe quite some things that are not there but which I took for granted. I wrote an article about this some time ago, I can send it to you, it was about that BW is the gray panther of DWing. Now to your comments: CDS views could carry maybe more semantic, but just the fact that the names of both views and fields are so long and in many cases almost self-explaining is a lot of semantics (for an e.g. Agent, I tested this). Just this is much better than previous MATNR or BUKRS which for German speaker migt still give a chance but for many other much harder to understand than material or company code. it's true that associations are not passed through, and that would be really cool, if the VDM of S4 could be selectively transfered. 

About "Only data physically stored in the object store can be shared with databricks using data products", fully agree, that is a little bit thin and DSP-based "data products" would need to be stored again in the object store, which is not really zero-copy. 
About "The idea of governed and semantically rich data is fine, but there is quite a long way before we are there." I agree, especially if we look outside. The BW extractors were by then the most important reason for making BW successful in its early years, apart of being part of NW. Many DW failed by then if running against SAP R/3. Now we have a much better understandable extraction logic with built in delta mechanism (_DEX CDS) and that's beautiful, for me at least. Having to create by yourself multi-table CDC on business entity level can be quite cumbersome without such infrastructure, and only a few tools, DSP included offer such a mechanism.

The main intention was to sum up pro's and con's or possible inhibitors of considering shift left approach, not only for SAP scenarios. I hope I have achieved this. Kind regards

entry.title: Sample value drivers for effective Business Value Realization of your Public Cloud Solution
entry.content:
In this blog, I continue with the topic of Business Value Realization. If you are reading this blog for the first time and you have not read my value series, then I suggest you look at these two blogs first to understand the full concept of value realization using the help of SAP for Me for SAP Public Cloud Solutions: Drive more Business Value out of your SAP Public Cloud Solution Subscriptions and SAP for Me – Adoption Report – Drive More Business Value Realization.

Here, I would like to focus on a simple example of how an SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) live customer can start with a quick, comprehensive analysis to add more business outcomes using some of the value drivers SAP provides in SAP for Me, with a high number of solution capabilities for SAP Cloud ERP.

Now, keep in mind these value drivers are not the only ones you can use, but I want to recommend them to you based on my experience and the fact they will help you improve multiple areas.

So for my example, I will use an SAP Cloud ERP customer utilizing finance, sales, procurement, logistics, and manufacturing.

Now, to simplify the analysis and example, I will only focus on two value drivers for the following improvement areas to make a comprehensive analysis:

  • Reduce selling, general, and administrative expenses
  • Increase revenue
  • Decrease cost of goods sold (COGS)
  • Improve working capital
  • Improve pre-tax margin

Sample value drivers by improvement area

Now, with these value drivers, I can go back to SAP for Me and use the Customer Insight Dashboard Cloud Functional Usage under the Business Value section to look at the solution areas my business can adapt to impact these value drivers more and bring greater business outcomes to your organization.

Let’s start looking at lowering our selling, general, and administrative expenses. Notice that after adding the value drivers “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate,” SAP for Me displays these results.

SAP for Me – Customer Insights Dashboard – Cloud Functional Usage – Business Value

Value Driver Selection - SAP for Me – Customer Insights Dashboard – Cloud Functional Usage – Business Value

Selling, general, and administrative expenses - “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate” value drivers

The customer is using 4 solution capabilities out of 10.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in selling, general and administrative expenses.

Our customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 6 additional solution capabilities. Of course, as previously discussed in my blogs, customers can compare the unused solution capabilities with the industry peer group to be more focused on the activation. All this information can be reached via SAP for Me.

Moving now to the Increase Revenue area and the value drivers “Reduce manufacturing cycle time” and “Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment Issues,” SAP for Me displays the results.

Revenue - “Reduce manufacturing cycle time” and “Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment Issues” value drivers

The customer is using 12 solution capabilities out of 15.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in revenue

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 3 additional solution capabilities.  

Within the Decreased Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) section, you’ll notice that when value drivers like “Reduce total manufacturing costs” and “Reduce asset maintenance cost” are added, SAP for Me displays the corresponding results.

Cost of goods sold - “Reduce total manufacturing costs” and “Reduce asset maintenance cost” value drivers

The customer is using 10 solution capabilities out of 15.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in cost of goods sold

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 5 additional solution capabilities.

When you add the value drivers "Reduce sales days outstanding" and "Reduce days in inventory" to improve the Working Capital area, SAP for Me displays the resulting outcomes.

Working capital – “Reduce sales days outstanding" and "Reduce days in inventory" value drivers

The customer is using 13 solution capabilities out of 30.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in working capital

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 17 additional solution capabilities.

If you add the value drivers of “Improved on-time delivery” and “Reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs”, SAP for Me shows the following results for improving the Pre-Tax Margin area.

Pre-Tax Margin - “Improved on-time delivery” and “Reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs” value drivers

The customer is using 20 solution capabilities out of 35.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in pre-tax margin

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 15 additional solution capabilities.

Now, summing it all up, to create a comprehensive analysis on additional value realization, here is the final view using the recommended value driver by improvement area. As you can see from the graphic, the yellow area provides us with an additional opportunity for value creation using solution capabilities currently not used, which can bring new improvements and impact the value drivers.

Additional opportunity for value creation in selected improvement areas

Now, realistically speaking, implementing all these solution capabilities can be arduous, so here I recommend thinking about adding another strategy, such as filtering by benchmarking with your industry peers, to truly reduce the scope and only select the ones that are proven to be heavily used by your industry peers. If you would like to see how to do this, refer to my previous blog "Drive more Business Value out of your SAP Public Cloud Solution Subscriptions."

Now, this example can also apply to other public cloud subscriptions like SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Integrated Business Planning, etc., so feel free to play with the value drivers included in SAP for Me to find the areas of improvement you want to drive for your business.

Keep in mind that solution capabilities are expanding from traditional functional scope to also include SAP Business AI, so SAP will continue to add more in this area. As in my previous blogs, remember, this is not the only tool that can be used; SAP has other tools such as SAP Signavio, SAP WalkMe, SAP LeanIX, SAP Value Lifecycle Manager, and/or SAP Cloud ALM that can be used in tandem to help drive more business value from your SAP subscription.

I encourage you to discuss these points with your SAP Customer Success Manager, SAP Account Executive, or SAP Partner. They may be able to provide you with additional recommendations and guidance.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia (SAP Business Suite Customer Success Management).

entry.title: HU Management for batch-managed raw materials: tracking partial quantities per container
entry.content:
We are working with a batch-managed raw material that is purchased and stored in physical containers (12 kg buckets).

At goods receipt, the material is posted in kilograms, so SAP correctly shows total stock and available batches. However, we currently have no visibility of:

  • how many physical buckets exist,

  • how many kg remain in each bucket after partial consumption,

  • and where each individual bucket is located.

Production often consumes only a partial quantity from a bucket, leaving open containers in the warehouse with different remaining quantities.

The business requirement is to track each physical bucket individually (container-level visibility), while keeping batch management for traceability and expiry purposes.

Questions:

  1. Is HU (Handling Unit) Management the recommended approach for this scenario?

  2. Can HU management be activated only for specific storage locations while the material remains batch-managed?

  3. How does SAP handle partial consumption of an HU and what happens when the HU quantity reaches zero?

  4. Which configuration or design considerations should be taken into account (IM - EWM)?

Any best practices or real-life implementation guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Enabling WebSocket for Fiori Elements app
entry.content:
Hi,

I managed to resolve this. It was two different issues and none of them was related to network.

Issue #1: when ws connection is accepted, there is a check for origin. Hence when doing local development, host localhost:8080 does not get accepted. The exceptions can be maintained in transaction SAPC_CROSS_ORIGIN. This is documented in OSS note and mentioned in documentation as well. This resolved the problem with local development. 

Issue #2: I looked at the requests in developer tools and I noticed the familiar pattern. The request to oData service would look like hostname/<site ID>.<app name>/<cache ID>/sap/opu... while request for websocket looked like hostname/sap/bc/apc/... I remembered this issue that for Workzone site, the URL path for service must be defined as a relative path instead of absolute path. The same problem was here. I debugged the creation of WS connection in Fiori Elements and it determined service path in absolute format /sap/bc/apc/... I found in UI5 documentation that it's possbile to adjust the websocket path via annotation. After adding the following annotation via local file it started working. Please note that the path is defined as relative, it does not start with /.

<Annotations Target="SAP__self.Container">
  <Annotation Term="Common.WebSocketBaseURL" String="sap/bc/apc/sap/odata" />
</Annotations>

It works all now. But my question would be, is this again just a limitation of Workzone or is there a better way how to make websocket works in Fiori Elements.

entry.title: Re: Unable to purchase any product in SAP Store as partne...
entry.content:
I still don't have authorizations on SAP Store, but we were able to access new applications on BTP, the issue was elsewhere. All solutions are available on SAP Marketplace, but the permission for entitlements on GlobalAccount must be given not only to the designated subaccount, but also the folder the subaccount is in. 

For instance, to provide access to 'Development CAP' inside 'CAP':

I have had to add the assignment in 'Entity Assignments' to both the subaccount and directory.

It has solved our issue and there was no need to access SAP Discovery Center.

entry.title: Quote 2.0 scripting: apply changes only to filtered rows/items in quote item grid?
entry.content:
Hi SAP CPQ community,

We are using SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 and want to apply a scripted update only to the items currently returned by the Quote UI “Filter Items” panel (Part Number / Product Type / Product Name filter)

Question:
Is there a supported way in Quote 2.0 to access the filtered result set (the rows/items currently shown after applying filters) from server-side scripting, so we can apply changes only to those items?

Context / what we tried:

  • In most cases our current logic applies changes across the quote using context.Quote.GetAllItems()
  • We tested whether the scripting context provides any “subset” collections (e.g., context.AffectedItems) but in our event context AffectedItems is not available (hasattr(context,"AffectedItems") == False)
  • We understand Quote 2.0 scripting is event-based and context objects vary by event.

If this isn’t possible directly from scripting, what’s the recommended pattern to “apply changes only to filtered lines” in Quote 2.0?

entry.title: Re: Story Reports: Display percentage underneath count in...
entry.content:
If there are only two values  in the dimension to a combined bar and line chart, you could use %GrandTotal to show both the actual number in each gender in the bars, plus the percentage as a line. However, if there are more than two values it doesn't work because it calculates each one based on the grand total. 

On the Formulas and Calculations page (link below), there's also a reference to a similar Subtotal option that theoretically should work, but I haven't been able to create a calculation using it. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/story-reports/all-formulas-and-calculations

entry.title: Returned Quantity Field Does Not Allow Decimal Values in Perform Maintenance Job App in FIORI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Please I have a question, In the Fiori app Perform Maintenance Job, the dialog Return Goods does not allow the entry of decimal values in the Returned Quantity field when returning components from a maintenance order.

The input field only accepts integer values, even for materials whose unit of measure supports decimal quantities.

 

 

Please someone have any solution?

Thank you,

Regards

entry.title: Variable Pay Basis Calculation does not populate following a change to Job Information in EC
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec

For our Variable Pay template, we run the following daily jobs to keep the employee data in variable pay up to date

  1. Import Employee History from Employee Central
  2. Trigger Basis Calculation
  3. Bonus Payout
  4. Update all worksheets

This is working well for the most part, however we have noticed that the Basis Calculation that populates Target Amount in Employee History is not calculating if an employee has had an EC change outside of the Bonus Start/End Dates. The only way to get the target amount to populate is to navigate to Employee History, tick that specific user and then click 'Update Selected'

Here are some more details below/an example.

  • Bonus Start Date: 01/07/2025
  • Bonus End Date: 31/12/2025
  • Worksheet Effective Date: 12/03/2026 (this is future dated so that employee details are current in the worksheet)
  • Global Eligibility Rule Effective Date: 19/01/2026 (this was the date we first ran the Import Employee History from Employee Central
  • Example employee on 29 January AM has a target amount of $1930, on 29 January PM the employee has a Line Manager change in EC effective 29 January, on 30 January AM when the above jobs run the target amount changes to zero. Try to trigger basis calc again but no change, only able to update by ticking this employee in the history and 'Update Selected'

Do you have any idea why EC changes outside of our Bonus Start and End Dates and impacting the ability for basis to calculate/populate?

Thank you!

entry.title: Field Service management - cloud audit logs service 404
entry.content:
I'm trying to connect to the cloud audit logs using the api but repeatedly get 404 errors when following the guide. 

I've removed my token and accountID but they're correct. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/cloud-audit-service.html


curl --location 'https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/cloud-audit-logs-service/api/audit/v1/account/{accountID}/logs?companyIds=-1' \--header 'Authorization: Bearer {bearerToken}' \--header 'X-Client-ID: admin' \--header 'X-Client-Version: 1.1' 

gives me the following response: 

{
    "timestamp": "2026-01-29T23:37:54.436+00:00",
    "status": 404,
    "error": "Not Found",
    "path": "/api/audit/v1/account/{accountID}/logs"
}

One thing from the linked documentation is, I'm not sure how to give the client SUPERUSER access since that's who is associated with the token.

entry.title: SAP B1 - Bin Setup
entry.content:
Hi,
I am trying to get bins setup in a test company.

I setup a warehouse for bins, and added locations.  I added inventory to a couple of items and put them in the bins.

I put in a sales order and created a pick list, when the pick list was created, the bins were auto assigned.

It there a way that I can generate a pick list but not have the bins setup, when the order is picked, they want to pick the bin.  On the printout, they want to see all the bin locations for each item on the pick list.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Brisbane - 2026 Kickoff!
entry.content:
Hey SAP friends and fellow Brissie tech enthusiasts!

We’re kicking off 2026 with something a little special — a relaxed, sun‑kissed, beach‑vibes‑themed SAP Stammtisch right here in Brisbane. Think chilled community catch‑ups, great conversations, new connections, and that easy‑going Queensland energy we all love.

Whether you’re deep in the SAP world, exploring new tools and technologies, building in the ecosystem, or simply curious about what others are doing — you’re invited.

🌺 What to Expect

🌊 A friendly, no‑slides, no‑pressure community meetup
🌺 Relaxed “beach vibes” atmosphere — tropical shirts encouraged!
🤝 Networking with SAP professionals, customers, partners & curious newcomers
💬 Open conversations about SAP BTP, Security, S/4HANA, careers, projects, challenges, and whatever else the group brings
🍹 Drinks and snacks available for purchase
😎 The perfect way to ease into the year and connect with your local SAP tribe

 

🌴 Who Should Come?

  • SAP practitioners
  • Customers & project teams
  • Partners & consultants
  • Students & career‑changers
  • Tech community members

If you’re part of the SAP ecosystem (or want to be), you’ll feel right at home.
